prose:
Method–provenance summary
• Property: frontier-orbital energies (HOMO, LUMO) and HOMO–LUMO gap (eV)
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting; exact four-center integrals)
• Geometry: user-supplied file `furan.xyz`, 9 atoms; no geometry optimisation – single-point calculation at this structure (notation: DFT//input)
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet, RKS)
• Software: PySCF (invoked via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill)
• Convergence: SCF converged in 9 cycles (scf_converged = true)
• Warnings: none reported
• Live engine log was streamed to a `frontier_*.out` file in the working directory during the run (path provided at run time); result JSON at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp5u3ynhhe/live.json`.

Key results (gas phase, eV)
• HOMO energy     = −6.3597638 eV
• LUMO energy     = −0.1024036 eV
• HOMO–LUMO gap   = 6.2573602 eV  ← headline value
• Vertical IP     = 6.3597638 eV  (Koopmans)
• Vertical EA     = 0.1024036 eV  (Koopmans)

The calculation therefore predicts a frontier-orbital gap of 6.2573602 eV for furan at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level in the gas phase, based on a PySCF single-point SCF that fully converged without warnings.
